content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 7, 2004

Tom Sykes, City Administrator
City of Commerce
City Hall
2535 Commerce Way
Commerce, CA 90040

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-186

Dear Mr. Sykes:

This letter confirms the receipt of your request for advice regarding an unnamed elected council member. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you identify the official and are writing as his/her authorized representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission can neither address your questions nor provide analysis. The FPPC may also decline to provide written advice, where an inquiry is hypothetical in nature (regulation 18329(b)(8)(D)).

I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review on the requirements for submitting an advice request. I have also included regulation 18702.5 (Public Identification of a Conflict of Interest for Section 87200 Filers) and Limitations and Restrictions on Gifts, Honoraria, Travel and Loans for locally elected officials; these documents may address some your concerns.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
JWW/jg

sections.question_synthetic: A City of Commerce administrator requested FPPC advice regarding an unnamed elected council member's conduct under the Political Reform Act, but failed to identify the specific official or provide authorization to act on their behalf.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request did not identify the specific elected official in question and was not submitted by an authorized representative, as required by California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC also noted the request appeared hypothetical in nature, which is grounds for declination under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal question or conclusion sections. Reasons for declination clearly stated in body text citing specific regulatory violations. Enclosures provided for requestor guidance on proper submission requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A City of Commerce administrator requested FPPC advice regarding an unnamed elected council member's conduct under the Political Reform Act, but failed to identify the specific official or provide authorization to act on their behalf.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request did not identify the specific elected official in question and was not submitted by an authorized representative, as required by California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC also noted the request appeared hypothetical in nature, which is grounds for declination under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).

The FPPC may also decline to provide written advice, where an inquiry is hypothetical in nature (regulation 18329(b)(8)(D)). I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review on the requirements for submitting an advice request. I have also included regulation 18702.5 (Public Identification of a Conflict of Interest for Section 87200 Filers) and Limitations and Restrictions on Gifts, Honoraria, Travel and Loans for locally elected officials; these documents may address some your concerns. embedding.summary: The FPPC declined an advice request from the City of Commerce regarding an unnamed council member's potential conflict of interest, citing failure to identify the specific official and lack of authorized representation as required by regulatory provisions.
